commentaries.author: Cornelius a Lapide
commentaries.content:
A Gentile: Gr. Î­Î»Î»Î®Î½Î¹Ï‚, i.e, a Grecian woman, for where the Greeks bore sway, all Gentiles were called Greeks. Hence the expression in the first chapter of the Epistle to the Romans , "The Jew first, and also the Greek" i.e, the Gentile.
A Syrophonician, i.e, belonging to that part of PhÅ“nicia which looks towards Syria.

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Have you not heard of the Syrophoenician woman? By the constancy of her entreaty, she elicited the Lord’s compassion. Homily , On Ephesians.

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content: For you, as women, have the very same angelic nature promised as your reward, the very same sexual respect as men. You have the same dignity in making moral judgments. This the Lord promises to women.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
